text1:
Hair loss is a major and distressing issue for many people around the world. Besides the physical changes, hair loss can lower your self-esteem. Additionally, it can impact overall quality of life.

In today’s world, you are lucky enough to restore your hairline through a hair transplant, thanks to the advancement in medical science. While there are various techniques available, Sapphire FUE hair transplant stands for its precision, non-invasive nature, and minimal downtime.

If you are looking to have a Sapphire hair transplant, Turkey is definitely the place to go. The country offers world-class doctors and modern facilities at a cost lower than most first-world nations, for example, USA, UK and Europe.

So, visit our Sapphire hair clinic, <color>Aslı Tarcan</color>, in Istanbul, Turkey. Here, skilled professionals will help you restore your hairline and confidence at little expense!
title1: What is Sapphire FUE Hair Transplant?
text2:
The classic Follicular Unit Extraction (FUE) is a modern hair transplant method that involves the extraction and implantation of hair follicles. This method, despite a high success rate, is constantly developing new innovations. One such example is the Sapphire technique.

In the Sapphire FUE process, a special blade, usually made from precious gemstone, is used instead of traditional metal blades. Sapphire blades are known for their sharpness and smoothness, opening precise channels on the donor and recipient area of your head.
title2: Benefits of Sapphire FUE Hair Transplant
text3: Advanced FUE hair transplant has gained immense popularity around the globe, mainly due to these reasons: • High Hair Density: Among all types of hair transplants, FUE sapphire hair transplant creates the most numbers of channels – 5000 FUE hair grafts. This means more hair can be transplanted, resulting in high hair density. • Reduced Trauma & Faster Healing: The sapphire blades are designed to be V-shaped. Due to the fact, the cuts that occur in Sapphire FUE are very narrow compared to conventional hair transplant methods. The result? Less damage to the scalp and quick healing of the transplanted area. • Natural Hair Growth: The blades involved in the procedure allow for the precise placement of hair follicles. With this accuracy, a natural-looking hairline is achieved. • High Success Rates: This advanced FUE hair transplant has a very favorable success rate (over 95%) because it is carried out with the highest possible precision. As a result, you can experience the greatest progress in hair density and coverage. • Enhanced Patient Satisfaction: FUE sapphire hair transplant is known to offer less invasive treatment, quicker recovery, and a natural-looking hairline. All these elements eventually lead to high patient satisfaction.
title3: Sapphire FUE Hair Transplant – A Step-by-Step Procedure
text4:
The Sapphire FUE (Follicular Unit Extraction), one of the best hair transplant techniques, is designed to achieve natural hair growth. Every stage, from initial consultation to post-surgery care, is essential.

Here’s what you can expect during the procedure:

<header>Step 1: Consultation</header>
The process starts with a full consultation where a specialist will assess your hair loss pattern, examine your scalp, and discuss your expectations. Your suitability for the Sapphire FUE procedure will also be determined.

<header>Step 2: Planning & Instructions</header>
Based on the consultation, the surgeon will plan the number of required hair grafts and design a hairline. This is necessary to achieve a natural look that suits your face.

<header>Step 3: Pre-Transplant Instructions</header>
You’ll also receive pre-transplant instructions regarding diet, medication, and scalp care to ensure you’re in optimal condition for the procedure.

<header>Step 4: Donor Area Preparation</header>
On the surgery day, the donor area (back of the scalp) will be shaved or trimmed. This allows the surgeon to access hair follicles easily.

<header>Step 5: Local Anesthesia</header>
Local anesthesia will be administered to the donor area for numbness before extraction starts.

<header>Step 6: Follicle Extraction</header>
Using a micro punch tool with a diameter of 0.7 to 1 mm, the hair transplant specialist will extract hair follicles. Following extraction, they will place the follicle units (grafts) in a special liquid solution (nutrient-rich liquid) to preserve them until transplantation.

<header>Step 7: Follicle Transplantation</header>
The trichologist will prepare the recipient area by making precise incisions with sapphire blades. Hair follicles are then implanted into these incisions with precision. The surgeon will ensure each follicle is placed at the right angle to mimic natural hair growth.

<header>Step 8: Aftercare</header>
After the treatment, the doctor will give you full instructions regarding scalp care such as medication to reduce discomfort and swelling, dietary restrictions, and sleeping positions. Follow-up appointments will be scheduled to check on the healing and hair growth.

<header>Step 9: Hair Growth</header>
Hair growth will start to show within a few months and more noticeable after 6 months. The full results of the transplant will be seen after 1 year.
title4: Who’s Ideal for Sapphire Hair Transplant?
text5: Sapphire FUE hair transplant is a great option for people experiencing hair loss and have enough hair follicles in their donor area. This procedure allows you to get back your lost hair and boost your confidence and looks. However, not everyone is an ideal candidate for this treatment. Prospective patients must be in generally good health and free from any active medical condition that can complicate the procedure or recovery. For example, they mustn’t have: • Active scarring alopecia • HIV • Hepatitis • Heart diseases • Diabetes • Lupus • Eczema Moreover, if you can’t tolerate local anesthesia for health reasons or allergies, then the process may not be suitable for you. It’s wise to consult with a doctor to assess your case and ensure the Sapphire blade hair transplant is good for you.
text6: No doubt, Sapphire FUE can give your head a nearly magical transformation. However, you must be aware of the aftermath during the recovery period: • Initial Discomfort: After the procedure, you can experience some slight discomfort or soreness in the area from where the hair is taken. You can avoid this using prescribed medicine or let it heal naturally. • Swelling and Redness: It is quite common for the patients to notice puffiness around the forehead and scalp. The transplant sites may also appear reddish, which can last for several days. This is a standard part of the body’s healing process and in most cases heals on its own. • Scabbing and Shedding: Approximately one week after the transplantation, fine scabs may appear on the implanted follicles. You must be very cautious not to scratch the scabs, as doing so can tear the healing grafts. • Shock loss: Hairs, which have been transplanted but not survived, will also fall out, mostly within the first one to four weeks. This is then known as “shock loss.” • Itching and Sensitivity: As the scalp undergoes the healing process, some patients may develop itching or feel overly sensitive in the treated areas. This feeling usually decreases with time, but applying mild shampoos can help relieve the uneasiness.
text7: Does Sapphire hair transplant leave scars? Sapphire FUE hair transplant leaves less scarring compared to traditional methods. Remaining small scars will fade as the hair grows over time. How many grafts can I get in one session? Number of grafts you'll receive in a single session depends on the donor area and extent of hair loss. Generally, you can get around 2,000 to 4,000 grafts in one session of Sapphire hair transplant. Your surgeon will assess your individual needs to determine the right number of grafts required for your case. Will the donor area be damaged after extracting hair from it? Donor area is not damaged if the extraction is done by a skilled surgeon using the right technique. Sapphire FUE ensures minimal trauma, reduced bleeding, and quick healing. Can I get a sapphire hair transplant without shaving my hair? In most cases, shaving is necessary to perform a Sapphire FUE transplant for better precision and graft extraction. Though some clinics offer unshaven procedures, this can make the process more challenging and may not give optimal results. Can Sapphire FUE Hair Transplantation Be Preferred For Women? Yes, women affected by hair thinning or mild to moderate hair loss can definitely prefer Sapphire FUE. The whole method is minimally surgical and results in a natural and dense hairline. Is One Session Enough to Achieve a Perfect Result? One session can give significant hair density but multiple sessions may be needed for full results depending on the extent of hair loss. Patients usually need a second session for fuller coverage or for larger areas. What is sapphire micro FUE hair transplant? In micro Sapphire FUE, micro Sapphire blades are utilized to create cuts on your head for grafts. These are even finer and precise than regular Sapphire blades to create small and precise incisions. What is the difference between micro FUE and sapphire FUE? Micro FUE is a procedure in which a manual or motorized punch is used to extract individual hair follicles, while Sapphire FUE uses a sapphire blade to gain a more accurate incision. Sapphire FUE is better as it offers more precision, less scarring, quicker recovery, and the ability to place the hair grafts in a better position. What is the recovery time for Sapphire FUE? Recovery time for Sapphire FUE is quick, most patients can get back to normal activities within 7 to 10 days. However, full healing and final results take 9 to 12 months and hair growth continues over time.
